notableMoments: Stunned Argentina 2-1 at the 2022 World Cup in one of the greatest upsets in tournament history, with goals from Saleh Al-Shehri and Salem Al-Dawsari
Saeed Al-Owairan scored one of the greatest World Cup goals ever in 1994, a mazy solo run against Belgium that is still celebrated as a defining moment in Saudi football
Reached the Round of 16 on their World Cup debut in 1994 in the USA, the best performance by a Saudi team at the tournament
Sami Al-Jaber became a Saudi legend by appearing in four consecutive World Cups (1994-2006), a record for the nation
The 2022 World Cup victory over Argentina was declared a national holiday in Saudi Arabia, underlining the cultural significance of the achievement
Suffered their heaviest World Cup defeat in 2002, losing 8-0 to Germany in the group stage in Japan and South Korea
Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 project has transformed football investment in the country, attracting global stars to the Saudi Pro League and raising the national team's profile
Have qualified for the 2026 World Cup as part of the AFC contingent, marking their seventh appearance at the tournament
